content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 27, 1990


James F. Roberts
Deputy General Counsel
Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
Box 54153
Los Angeles, CA  90054

                                Re:  Conflict of Interest Code for the
                                     Metropolitan Water District of
                                     Southern California
                                     Our File No. A-90-156

Dear Mr. Roberts:

     In response to our conversation of January 5, 1990, I am
writing this letter to reiterate staff's position with respect to
the validity of provisions of the Metropolitan Water District
conflict-of-interest code in light of the disclosure requirements
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1  Thank you for your
patience in conjunction with this matter.

     The specific provision at issue is contained in section
7200(b) of your district's conflict-of-interest code.  That sec-
tion provides as follows:

          Except as provided in Section 7200(d)
     investments in business entities and sources of
     income are not reportable if the reported annual
     gross income and assets of the business entity or
     sources of income each exceeded 250 million dollars
     for the year preceding the filing of any financial
     disclosure statement under this Code.

     Section 7200(d) of your district's code provides that certain
investments and sources of income are reportable in any event if
they are private water companies, public utilities selling

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File No. A-90-156
Page 2

electric power to the district or companies providing "(a)
construction services utilized in water activities, (b) electrical
equipment and supplies, and (c) engaged in real estate development, and (d) classified as large water users."

Section 87302 of the Act provides, in part, as follows:

Each Conflict of Interest Code shall contain
the following provisions:

(a) Specific enumeration of the positions
within the agency, other than those specified in
Section 87200, which involve the making or
participation in the making of decisions which may
foreseeably have a material effect on any financial
interest and for each such enumerated position, the
specific types of investments, ... and sources of
income which are reportable. An investment, ... or
source of income shall be made reportable by the
Conflict of Interest Code if the business entity in
which the investment ... is held, ... or income
or source of income may foreseeably be affected
materially by any decision made or participated in
by the designated employee by virtue of his or her
position.

(b) ... The information disclosed with
respect to reportable investments, interests in
real property, and income shall be the same as the
information required by Sections 87206 and 87207.

(Emphasis added.)

Section 87302 requires that a conflict-of-interest code
specify the types of investments and sources of income which are
reportable. The filer must have received $250 or more from the
source of income and must have an investment interest of $1,000 or
more in the business entity. The filer must make or participate
in making decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect
on the business entity which is a source of income or an investment. Once it has been determined that particular types of
investments and sources of income are reportable, the Act does not
provide for limiting the disclosure of this information based upon
the annual assets or income of a business entity which otherwise
qualifies for disclosure.

In 1985, the Commission adopted Regulation 18702.1 (copy
enclosed), which provides, in part, that the effect of a decision
is material if the business entity in which the official has an
interest or which is a source of income to the official is directly
involved in a decision before the official's agency, unless the
decision will have no financial effect upon the business entity.
Thus, it is foreseeable that a business entity which has assets

Our File No. A-90-156
Page 3

and annual income in excess of $250 million could be materially
affected by a decision if the entity is directly involved. This
could occur, for example, if the decision were whether to contract
with the entity.

Therefore, once it is determined that a person is a
designated employee subject to disclosure under a conflict of
interest code and that the business entity is a source of income
or investment of the type which must be disclosed, disclosure must
be made. Disclosure cannot be further limited by the size of the
business entity. Therefore, we again advise that the provision of
your district's code providing for exemption for business entities with assets and income in excess of $250 million be deleted.

If you have any questions regarding the above, you may
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Margaret W. Ellison
    Counsel, Legal Division

KED:MWE:aa

Encl.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Metropolitan Water District's conflict-of-interest code provision exempting investments and sources of income exceeding $250 million in annual assets and gross income from disclosure requirements complies with the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that the $250 million exemption provision must be deleted because once an investment or source of income is determined to be reportable under the Political Reform Act, disclosure cannot be further limited based on the size of the business entity, even if it exceeds $250 million in assets or annual income.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains a clear legal question and definitive FPPC position. The document is structured as a formal advice letter in response to prior conversation. File number listed as A-90-156 in header and A-90-155 on page 2 (appears to be typographical inconsistency in source document).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the Metropolitan Water District's conflict-of-interest code provision exempting investments and sources of income exceeding $250 million in annual assets and gross income from disclosure requirements complies with the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advises that the $250 million exemption provision must be deleted because once an investment or source of income is determined to be reportable under the Political Reform Act, disclosure cannot be further limited based on the size of the business entity, even if it exceeds $250 million in assets or annual income.

The specific provision at issue is contained in section 7200(b) of your district's conflict-of-interest code. That sec- tion provides as follows: Except as provided in Section 7200(d) investments in business entities and sources of income are not reportable if the reported annual gross income and assets of the business entity or sources of income each exceeded 250 million dollars for the year preceding the filing of any financial disclosure statement under this Code. Section 7200(d) of your district's code provides that certain investments and sources of income are reportable in any event if they are private water companies, public utilities selling 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. Section 87302 of the Act provides, in part, as follows: Each Conflict of Interest Code shall contain the following provisions: (a) Specific enumeration of the positions within the agency, other than those specified in Section 87200, which involve the making or participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect on any financial interest and for each such enumerated position, the specific types of investments, ... and sources of income which are reportable. An investment, ... or source of income shall be made reportable by the Conflict of Interest Code if the business entity in which the investment ... is held, ... or income or source of income may foreseeably be affected materially by any decision made or participated in by the designated employee by virtue of his or her position. (b) ... The information disclosed with respect to reportable investments, interests in real property, and income shall be the same as the information required by Sections 87206 and 87207. (Emphasis added.) Section 87302 requires that a conflict-of-interest code specify the types of investments and sources of income which are reportable. The filer must have received $250 or more from the source of income
embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to the Metropolitan Water District regarding the validity of a conflict-of-interest code provision that exempts reportable investments and sources of income when the business entity exceeds $250 million in annual assets and gross income. The FPPC advises that this exemption violates the Political Reform Act and must be removed.
